text.title: Blameless Retro Template (Human Facilitation) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounded in SRE / incident-response literature (Etsy, PagerDuty, Sidney Dekker, Edmondson).

rule.statement: Facilitator MUST interrupt blame language in real-time. Person-as-subject phrases ('Bob deployed') are reframed as system-as-subject ('the deploy pipeline allowed') in the moment, not after.
rule.rationale: Post-meeting language cleanup does not change participant behaviour. Real-time reframing teaches the pattern.

rule.statement: Retro MUST identify ≥3 contributing factors. Single-cause 'root cause' framing is forbidden — incidents always have multiple contributing factors.

rationale.source: Sidney Dekker — The Field Guide; John Allspaw — How Complex Systems Fail

rule.rationale: Single-root-cause framing produces single-fix action items that don't transfer learning. Multi-factor analysis surfaces detection, comms, mitigation gaps separately.

rule.statement: Every action item MUST have a single named owner AND a target date ≤90 days. 'The team will look into' = wish, not action.
rule.rationale: Unowned action items complete at <15%; owned + dated at 60-75% per organisational research.

rule.statement: Final agenda block is the META round (10 min, non-skippable): 'What did we ALMOST learn from this incident but didn't dig into?'

rationale.source: Liminal Thinking; Edmondson on organisational learning

rule.rationale: Meta round catches the highest-leverage insights — the things people noticed but didn't say. Without explicit space they die.

rule.statement: Retro record MUST be published to a known location (wiki, repo/postmortems) within 48 hours, with all action items + owners + dates visible to a broader audience than meeting participants.
rule.rationale: Published records are auditable, searchable, citeable. Unpublished retros become tribal knowledge that decays.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
